title: QA Eisenstein norm flip under T — signed-temporal structure of T-orbits on S_9
_fixture_purpose: Certifies the integer identity f(e, b+e) = -f(b, e) where f(b,e) = b*b + b*e - e*e is the Eisenstein quadratic form. Corollary: T^2 preserves f mod m. On S_9 the five T-orbits decompose into 3 signed cosmos orbits (norm pairs {1,8}/{4,5}/{2,7}), 1 null satellite orbit, 1 null singularity. This is the signed-temporal view of the same T dynamic certified structurally by [211]-[213].
source_attribution: Gotthold Eisenstein, 'Untersuchungen ueber die cubischen Formen mit zwei Variabeln' (Journal fuer die reine und angewandte Mathematik 27, 1844) — foundational work on binary/ternary quadratic forms with determinant-1 linear action. Pythagorean Families paper (Will Dale + Claude, 2026-03) provides the 5-orbit classification with classical names (Fibonacci, Lucas, Phibonacci, Tribonacci, Ninbonacci). Will Dale + Claude, 2026-04-11.
theorem_statement: The Eisenstein quadratic form f(b, e) = b*b + b*e - e*e satisfies the integer identity f(e, b+e) = -f(b, e) for all (b, e) in Z^2. Consequently, the Fibonacci matrix F = [[0,1],[1,1]] acting on Z^2 sends f to -f, so F^2 preserves f. On S_9, T^2 preserves f mod 9, and the 5 T-orbits decompose into signed pairs and null subgraphs.
proof: Direct polynomial expansion: f(e, b+e) = e*e + e*(b+e) - (b+e)*(b+e) = e*e + b*e + e*e - (b*b + 2*b*e + e*e) = e*e - b*e - b*b = -(b*b + b*e - e*e) = -f(b, e). Each step uses only ring-of-integers identities; the result holds over any commutative ring. Corollary: applying T twice gives f(T^2(b,e)) = -f(T(b,e)) = -(-f(b,e)) = f(b,e). QED.

signed_orbits_s9.note: Primary Fibonacci cosmos orbit; norm pair {1, -1 mod 9} = {1, 8}. 12 states with norm 1, 12 with norm 8, alternating under T.
Lucas cosmos orbit; norm pair {4, -4 mod 9} = {4, 5}. Bipartite signed with period 2.
Phibonacci cosmos orbit; norm pair {2, -2 mod 9} = {2, 7}. Bipartite signed.
Satellite orbit; Eisenstein norm identically 0 mod 9 because b, e both divisible by 3 gives f(b,e) = 9*(b'*b' + b'*e' - e'*e') divisible by 9. 'Null' subgraph.
Singularity fixed point; f(9,9) = 81 + 81 - 81 = 81 which is 0 mod 9. Null subgraph.

temporal_sign_formula: On the Z^2 integer lift (not mod-reduced), sign(f(T^t(s_0))) = (-1)^t * sign(f(s_0)). On mod-9 orbits the norm values split into a bipartite {+k, -k} = {+k, m-k} coloring with period 2.
connection_to_bearden_155: Phase conjugation in Bearden's scalar EM framework is structurally parallel to norm-sign flip: global QCI+ and local QCI- are the two halves of a sign-paired orbit. [155] certifies the empirical QCI opposite-sign discovery; this cert [214] identifies the underlying algebraic mechanism as the Eisenstein norm flip f(T(s)) = -f(s).

witnesses.note: Norm alternates between +1 and -1 on integer lift, matching the theorem. On mod-9 these correspond to norms {1, 8}.
f(1,3)=1+3-9=-5 (-5 mod 9 = 4). After T: f(3,4)=9+12-16=5 wait this should be -5 mod 9 = 4, let me recompute: (3,4) is state after T(1,3), f(3,4)=9+12-16=5 mod 9 = 5. So the pair is {4, 5} confirmed.
f(3,3) = 9+9-9 = 9 = 0 mod 9. Satellite orbit has identically null norm. Any two satellite states differ by 3-divisibility, so f stays in the ideal (9) of Z.
f(9,9) = 81+81-81 = 81 = 0 mod 9. Fixed point under T, single null state.
